rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the component type scope for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Preserve existing AADL architecture by not modifying component types or implementations without necessity
Ensure all referenced identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the target scope
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use component type annexes for component-wide contracts and implementation annexes for implementation-specific contracts
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes for port references
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not place input envelope assumptions or output range guarantees in component type annexes without concrete bounds or visible relations
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
